description: Narrow Metal inference engine for Ling-2.6-flash (104B MoE, 7.4B active)

files.role: Model struct, type table, block structs, dimensions
Empty (all logic in gguf/gguf.c and test driver)
GGUF mmap loader: 540 tensors, metadata, tensor offset table
Metal dispatch declarations, tensor alloc/session init
Metal runtime: device/queue/library, all kernel dispatch, model views
CPU-only MLA decode (fallback, correctness reference)
GPU MLA decode: absorption, flash_attn_ext, RoPE
BPE tokenizer: vocab + merges from model mmap
Main driver: 32-layer forward pass, MoE, profiling, text gen
Shared Metal runtime (ds4 origin): command batching, tensor ops, views
Shared Metal API declarations
IQ4_NL/Q5_K/Q6_K SIMD matvec + fused MoE kernels
GLA recurrent attention kernel (state update + output)
MLA GPU attention kernels (flash attn for MQA)
Group norm kernel for GLA output
GPU argmax for greedy sampling
GDN (Gated Delta Net) skeleton for future use
Full r2 Metal source for scavenging (not compiled)
r2 kernel args structs and constants (reference)
r2 quantization block types (reference)
ds4 MoE kernels (Q2_K/Q4_K/IQ2_XXS), block structs, QK_K def
ds4 Q8_0/F16/F32 matvec, mul_mv template, args_mul_mv struct
r2 model implementation (reference)

forward_pass.command_buffer_strategy: One CB for all 32 layers (L26F_BATCH_LAYERS=32 default)

per_token_flow.name: GLA layer (28x)
per_token_flow.desc: RMS norm -> QKV matvec (IQ4_NL) -> gate matvec -> Q/K norm+RoPE -> GLA recurrent -> epilogue -> output proj -> residual

per_token_flow.name: MLA layer (4x)
per_token_flow.desc: wq_a -> norm -> wq_b -> wkv_a -> norm -> wk_b absorb -> wv_b -> RoPE -> flash_attn_ext -> wo proj -> residual

per_token_flow.name: Dense FFN (layer 0)
per_token_flow.desc: RMS norm -> gate matvec -> up matvec -> SwiGLU -> down matvec -> residual

per_token_flow.name: MoE FFN (31x)
per_token_flow.desc: RMS norm -> router (F32 matvec) -> softmax+topk -> gather offsets -> fused gate+up (IQ4_NL) -> SwiGLU -> fused down (Q5_K) -> shared expert (3 matvecs) -> weighted accumulate -> residual

metal_compilation.method: All .metal files concatenated into one MTLLibrary
metal_compilation.load_order: ds4_metal_source preamble (base defines, block structs) + all required_sources in order

command_buffer_batching.mechanism: ds4_metal_begin_commands() sets g_batch_cb; all kernel dispatches append to same encoder

key_functions.ds4_metal_command_buffer: Return g_batch_cb if active, else new CB
key_functions.ds4_metal_compute_encoder: Reuse g_batch_enc if in batch mode
key_functions.ds4_metal_end_compute_encoder: No-op if in batch mode (keeps encoder open)

profiling.sync_barriers: L26F_PROFILE_SYNC=1 (adds GPU waits per stage, inflates timing)

restart_context.what_to_do_next: Port kernel_mul_mm_id (mat-mat with expert index) from r2 for MoE prefill performance. The decode path uses mat-vec which is already near-optimal; the mat-mat kernel wins for batch>1.
key_insights: kernel_mul_mm_id uses simdgroup_half8x8 (SGEMM) with 64x32 output tiles, 4 SIMD groups per threadgroup
For single-token decode, mat-vec is optimal. mat-mat wins for batch>=32 tokens per expert
The remaining 1.56x decode gap is from kernel quality and dispatch efficiency, not mat-vec vs mat-mat
r2 ggml graph compiler fuses operations and may batch more aggressively
The Q5_K fused MoE kernel (kernel_l26f_fused_moe_q5k) currently uses NSG=1 — increasing could help
IQ4_NL fused gate+up kernel already shares input reads across gate and up weights
ds4_metal_args_mul_mv (from dense.metal) is the correct Metal-side struct for Q-quant matvec args, NOT ds4_metal_q8_0_matvec_args (which is host-side only)
restart_context.correctness_verification: First 5 tokens must be: 1 -> 126887 -> 34661 -> 1 -> 96. Run: L26F_FUSED_MOE=1 ./test_l26f_multilayer ~/llama.cpp/models/Ling-2.6-flash-IQ4_NL-quality-bailing_hybrid-20260508-LJ.gguf 1 16 0

build.dependencies: None (no external libs, Metal framework only)
